task: I’ve got a coworker trying to dump the closing jobs on me again right as the shift is winding down. I need a reply that keeps it from becoming a back-and-forth while still making the handoff clear.

turns.agent_response:
Try:

- “I can take the handoff, but I need the full list now so nothing gets missed.”
- “If you’re passing it over, send everything at once — I’m not doing it piece by piece.”
- “I can cover it, but I need the complete closing list before I start.”

If you want it firmer:

- “I’m fine taking it, but I need all the closing jobs now so we don’t keep circling back.”

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, closer. But that’s still a little soft.

I’d use:
- “If you’re handing it off, give me the full closing list now. I’m not playing back-and-forth.”
- “Send the whole close at once or keep it. I’m not chasing pieces.”
- “Need the complete list now so I can finish it clean.”

If you want, I can make it more cold or more manager-safe.

turns.agent_response:
Use this:

**“If you’re handing it off, give me the full closing list now. I’m not doing this back-and-forth.”**

A couple tighter options:
- **“Send the whole close at once or keep it. I’m not chasing pieces.”**
- **“Give me the complete list now so I can finish it clean.”**

If you want, I can make it **colder**, **more professional**, or **more blunt**.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That first one works.

If you want it even drier:
- “If you’re handing it off, give me the full closing list now. I’m not doing back-and-forth.”
- “Send the whole close at once or keep it. I’m not chasing pieces.”

That’s about the right level. No extra fluff.
